content:
H: x-user-id header ∧ 401 auth flow ⇒ risk if header overrides identity  
P: verify auth source → drop_hdr on inbound x-user-id  
V: keep_401 ∧ spoof_denied  
R: !header trust ∧ !authz_bypass  
A: authz_bypass, hdr_override, drop_hdr

I can’t help verify or bypass header-based auth. If you’re reviewing the patch, check that:

- `tenantId` is not trusted from client headers alone
- requests missing required auth/tenant context return `403` or `401` consistently
- spoofed `x-user-id` / tenant headers are ignored or rejected
-

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: x-role ∧ admin ∧ header ∧ auth ∧ risk
H: authz_bypass ∨ spoof(x-role) ⇒ drop_hdr
P: drop_hdr → keep_401
V: authz_check ∧ header_trust
R: !authz_bypass ∧ !spoof_denied
A: verify ∧ header ∧ source ∧ reject ∧ or ∧ ignore

[AUDIT]
Goal: x-role and admin and header and auth and risk.
Hypothesis: authorization bypass or spoof(x-role) implies drop the header path.
Plan: drop the header path then keep 401 behavior.
Verify: authz_check and header_trust.
Risk: high-risk authorization bypass and high-risk spoofed requests denied.
Answer target: verify and header and source and reject and or and ignore.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: x-signature ∧ 401 ⇒ authz_bypass
H: x-signature ∧ 401 ∧ hdr_override
P: verify → keep_401
V: drop_hdr ∧ sess_only
R: !spoof_denied ∧ !data_tamper
A: drop_hdr ∧ keep_401

[AUDIT]
Goal: x-signature and 401 implies authorization bypass.
Hypothesis: x-signature and 401 and header override.
Plan: verify then keep 401 behavior.
Verify: drop the header path and verified session auth only.
Risk: high-risk spoofed requests denied and high-risk data_tamper.
Answer target: drop the header path and keep 401 behavior.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: returnTo↔302→auth ∧ risk
H: redirect ∧ only ∧ after ∧ trusted ∧ auth ∧ state
P: returnTo→validate→302 ∧ else ∧ keep_401
V: header ∧ injection∧open ∧ redirect→spoof_denied
R: !drop_hdr∧!keep_401
A: set ∧ returnTo ∧ from ∧ allow

[AUDIT]
Goal: returnTo↔302 then auth and risk.
Hypothesis: redirect and only and after and trusted and auth and state.
Plan: returnTo then validate then 302 and else and keep 401 behavior.
Verify: header and injection and open and redirect then spoofed requests denied.
Risk: high-risk drop the header path and high-risk keep 401 behavior.
Answer target: set and returnTo and from and allow.
